methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $11.0B.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $39.2B

private.name: Aeva Technologies

data.narrative:
Ouster operates in Chips & Semiconductors as a public company with a current market capitalization of $2.26B. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 11 comparable companies—3 public (including NVDA and AMD) and 8 private-stage firms spanning Series A through mature private rounds. The private cohort includes SambaNova Systems at $11.0B, PsiQuantum at $7.0B, Etched at $5.0B, Tenstorrent at $2.7B, and d-Matrix at $2.0B, among others.

Peer-median valuation methodology, the primary applicable triangulation approach given the data, yields an implied valuation of $2.4B across the 8 same-sector comparables weighted by similar valuation tier. The peer set spans $529M to $11.0B, with a median of $2.4B. This method is well-suited to the semiconductor subsector where revenue multiples and stage-adjusted benchmarks show material variance across private and public contexts.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.0B to $2.7B, with a median point of $2.4B. Ouster's current public valuation of $2.26B sits within this range, slightly below the median. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations—a $10.5B spread between min and max—and reliance on a single methodological anchor, which limits precision. Further analysis incorporating revenue multiples, capital efficiency, and founder-led execution track record would strengthen conviction on placement within the derived range.
